g.data-narration: Welcome to this lecture on M2M versus IoT. These two terms are often confused but represent fundamentally different connectivity paradigms.
g.text: M2M vs IoT: Understanding the Difference

g.data-narration: Machine-to-Machine (M2M) enables direct communication between devices without human intervention. It uses point-to-point connections, often in closed systems.
The Internet of Things (IoT) is broader - it connects entire ecosystems of devices over the internet, enabling cloud integration and intelligent data analytics.
g.text: • IP-based network connectivity over the internet

g.data-narration: M2M architecture is hardware-centric with direct communication. A vending machine sends data directly to a distributor server via cellular network.
IoT architecture is cloud-centric with multiple data streams. Smart devices send data to the internet, where cloud platforms integrate and analyze it for intelligent decisions.
M2M uses legacy protocols like Zigbee and proprietary standards. IoT uses IP-based protocols like HTTP, MQTT, and CoAP for global accessibility.
M2M uses data for immediate tasks with limited storage. IoT sends data to the cloud for big data analytics, predictive insights, and machine learning.
M2M systems are siloed and proprietary with low interoperability. IoT is designed for horizontal integration across different verticals using APIs.
M2M devices follow pre-defined rules with dumb connectivity. IoT supports automation, machine learning, and predictive actions based on data analysis.
g.text: IF sensor greater than threshold THEN alert

g.data-narration: M2M is hardware-oriented and monolithic. IoT is software-defined, cloud-centric, and layered with clear separation between devices, network, and applications.
Cloud computing, big data analytics, SDN, NFV, and NETCONF-YANG enable scalable IoT systems beyond simple M2M communication.
Remember: M2M is machine connectivity for specific purposes. IoT interconnects entire ecosystems over the internet for intelligent, data-driven decision-making.
For exams: Remember IoT must involve internet and IP networking. M2M is about single action from single data. Use examples to illustrate answers.
g.text: • Simple differentiator: IoT MUST involve internet/IP; M2M might not
• M2M = single action from single data point; IoT = aggregating data for predictive analysis
• Link to SDN/NFV, NETCONF-YANG from Module 2
